name: Elevation / DEM layer (SF hills)
role: Supply elevation so SF's hills are real: drape the road network over a Digital Elevation Model to get a z per node and a grade per edge.
decision: Pull a DEM (USGS 3DEP 1m/10m, or SRTM 30m) for the SF bbox. Sample it at each OSMnx node -> node z; finite-difference along edges -> per-edge grade. Feed grade into BOTH layers: road-mesh-builder drapes roads on the DEM for a 2.5D hilly Isaac mesh, and the kinematic env uses grade as a speed/accel modifier + an obs scalar so coordination accounts for hill timing.
license: USGS 3DEP public domain; SRTM public domain
why_both_layers: Physical: terrain mesh in Isaac so the car physically climbs/descends; WheeledLab's elevation task = the slope-handling low-level skill.
Behavioral: uphill is slower / downhill needs longer braking -> grade changes timing, which the coordination policy must see (scalar obs) to plan.
risks: DEM resolution vs OSM positional noise -> smooth grades
draping introduces z-discontinuities at junctions -> clean before meshing
Isaac USD terrain collider pitfalls (objects falling through) -> verify contacts
effort: 1 day (sampling + grade) + meshing time on the Isaac side
